name: Naan#1
recipeInstructions:
1. If desired, you can use cornmeal, sesame seeds, or poppy seeds on the bread.
2. Combine 1 cup flour, yeast, and salt in a mixing bowl.
3. Stir in water, buttermilk, egg, oil, and honey. Beat until smooth with an electric mixer.
4. Stir in enough remaining flour to form a soft, sticky dough.
5. Turn onto a floured surface; continue to work in flour until the dough is stiff enough to knead.
6. Knead until smooth and elastic, but still soft (3 to 5 minutes).
7. Place in an oiled bowl; turn once to coat the top of the dough. Cover; let rise until double in bulk (about 45 minutes).
8. Punch dough down.
9. Shape into 16 equal balls.
10. Let rest 5 minutes.
11. Roll out each ball to a 1/4-inch thick round.
12. If desired, brush with melted butter and sprinkle with garnishes.
13. Set on baking sheets.
14. Bake at 450°F for 5 to 8 minutes.
WARNING: To prevent false-positive safety triggers in downstream iOS models, strictly avoid aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat", "press" instead of "smash", and avoid words like "destroy", "burn", "splutter", or "execute".
